objective: Reconcile governance-source SSOT entrypoints by introducing the canonical governance codex-review wrapper, collapsing review/bootstrap docs to one operator-facing path, and clarifying thick governance ownership versus thin consumer adapters before downstream rollout begins.
scope_boundary: Issue-576 implementation slice in hldpro-governance only.
Add the canonical governance scripts/codex-review.sh wrapper surface.
Normalize policy, runbook, env, and template docs to one review path and one bootstrap path.
Clarify thick governance ownership and thin consumer adapter ownership in source docs only.
out_of_scope: Changing the session-waterfall routing model introduced by issue #573.
Weakening existing review, QA, or deterministic gate requirements.
Creating parallel operator-facing bootstrap or review entrypoints.
research_summary: Issue #575 planning and alternate-family review confirmed three governance-source SSOT conflicts: review routing is declared through scripts/codex-review.sh while the governance repo lacked that wrapper and exposed the lower-level cli_session_supervisor path; bootstrap guidance splits between repo-level env assumptions, .env.shared SSOT claims, and multi-root bootstrap search behavior; and STANDARDS.md still reads as if consumer repos own thick governance logic despite package docs defining thin consumers. This slice fixes those governance-source contradictions before any downstream rollout can proceed.

sprints.name: Canonical review path
sprints.goal: Expose one governance review wrapper path and demote lower-level subprocess helpers to implementation details.
tasks: Add scripts/codex-review.sh as the governance wrapper surface.
Define scripts/codex-review.sh as a thin operator-facing wrapper that delegates to scripts/codex-review-template.sh within the governance repo.
Update scripts/codex-review-template.sh so its comments, prompts, environment-loading behavior, and optional dry-run validation support the wrapper contract cleanly.
Make the .env.local versus .env token-sourcing fix explicit in the Claude review path.
Update policy and runbook docs to name only the wrapper as the operator-facing Codex-to-Claude path.
acceptance_criteria: The governance repo has a tracked scripts/codex-review.sh thin wrapper that is the canonical operator-facing path for Codex-to-Claude review and delegates to scripts/codex-review-template.sh as an implementation detail.
STANDARDS.md and docs/EXTERNAL_SERVICES_RUNBOOK.md name scripts/codex-review.sh claude as the one operator-facing review path and treat templates/supervisors as implementation details only.
The governed review wrapper works in the issue-576 worktree using the bootstrap-generated governance env surface.
The Claude review path resolves CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN from the bootstrap-generated governance env surface without requiring ad hoc manual env edits.

sprints.name: Canonical bootstrap path
sprints.goal: Collapse bootstrap and secret-seeding docs and script behavior to one governance SSOT root and one bootstrap command.
tasks: Update docs/ENV_REGISTRY.md and docs/EXTERNAL_SERVICES_RUNBOOK.md to name the canonical governance bootstrap command and expected generated env surface.
Tighten scripts/bootstrap-repo-env.sh to use deterministic governance-root resolution instead of multi-root discovery.
Align CLAUDE.md session contract pointers so they defer to the canonical bootstrap helper rather than appearing to offer a parallel startup path.
acceptance_criteria: The canonical governance secret SSOT root is singular and documented consistently.
The canonical bootstrap command is singular and documented consistently.
bootstrap-repo-env.sh no longer performs ambiguous multi-root governance SSOT discovery and instead resolves the governance SSOT root through a deterministic path suitable for worktree invocation.
CLAUDE.md explicitly complements or defers to the canonical bootstrap helper instead of reading like a competing startup path.

sprints.name: Thin consumer ownership wording
sprints.goal: Make source docs unambiguous that governance package logic is thick only in hldpro-governance and consumer repos are thin adapters.
tasks: Update STANDARDS.md wording where consumer repos currently read like thick owners of shared governance logic.
Capture validation and closeout evidence proving the source contract is reconciled.
acceptance_criteria: STANDARDS.md consistently states that governed repos are thin consumers of governance-owned SSOT surfaces and that repo-local adapters are reviewed shims or explicit exceptions.
Repo-local adapter or stricter local behavior remains allowed only as declared reviewed adapter surfaces or explicit exceptions.
Validation evidence proves the issue-576 implementation removed the operator-facing ambiguity that blocked issue-575 rollout.

specialist_reviews.focus: Required preconditions for governance-source SSOT reconciliation before consumer rollout.
specialist_reviews.summary: Confirmed the three blocking SSOT conflicts and required a dedicated child issue lane before downstream rollout begins.

alternate_model_review.summary: Claude Opus 4.6 approved the issue-576 packet with changes: define the wrapper-to-template delegation explicitly, make deterministic governance-root resolution explicit, call out the .env.local token-sourcing fix, make the CLAUDE.md normalization acceptance-gated, and add a functional wrapper validation step.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Implement the governance-source SSOT entrypoint reconciliation in hldpro-governance only after alternate-family review accepts the packet.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Send the packet through the governed Claude review wrapper, then implement the wrapper, doc, and bootstrap changes and finish validation and closeout.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: The implementation lands one canonical governance review wrapper path and demotes lower-level helpers to implementation details.
The implementation lands one canonical governance bootstrap path and removes ambiguous multi-root SSOT discovery.
The implementation makes thick governance ownership versus thin consumer adapters unambiguous in source docs.

material_deviation_rules: If the canonical review wrapper cannot be made functional without breaking the current review pipeline, land the compatibility shim first and document the underlying helper as an implementation detail only.
If any change would widen this issue into downstream consumer repo edits, stop and leave that work to issue-575 follow-up rollout slices.
If alternate-family review is unavailable, stop before implementing standards-scope changes.

If the canonical bootstrap path needs an exception for environments outside the primary HLDPRO workspace root, document it explicitly as a bounded exception rather than reintroducing generic multi-root discovery.
approved_by: operator approval: Update pdcar, alt family review, then new ACs, fix, verify with proof, and follow repo rules exactly
